channel.title: Pocket Realms
channel.generator: Webpage HTML Export plugin for Obsidian

item.title: Corona
item.description:
The surface of Corona is vibrant and colorful, light itself seems to playfully dance into different colors.The ground and fungal forests shift and flow, the environments themselves not staying the same for more than a few days, going from a raging volcano to an ocean to an arctic plain (as an example).Going beneath the surface of the world, mana naturally and readily crystallizes into veins, more common than most metals.
Further down lies the outer skin of the realm's Engine. The entire place has undergone concretion, being covered in layers of hard rock, to the point that it only vaguely resembles a melty facility.Surrounding the stellar core is a cloud of iridescent rainbow dust.Their equatorial band is a scar on the world, a wounded and jagged beige that does not shift with the area around it. Standing there however, you would see ripples of sand like those beneath shallow water, then fifty or so feet in each direction, nothing but mirages. Perpetually standing on an island of sand in the middle of an endless ocean.The poles are white caps that reflect the perpetual auroras that bathe their skies.
The Splinters of Corona are emotionally limited, as they all are, but they are limited to the following emotions;
Love
They can feel nothing else, and as such are difficult for Humans to properly comprehend. For they love everyone and everything, they have a fondness and affection for everything, but how it manifests can vary wildly. From simple friendships, to romantic or sexual interest, to altruistic self sacrifices of Agape or violent selfishness of Yandere, similarly, they can bleed into other forms of emotions such as hatred and sorrow and grief. Regardless of manifestation, they care, deeply, they love, but such an unconditional mentality is alien to most Humans.The Humans of Corona are highly militarized, service is required for citizenship, the more tours, the higher their status and privileges within their society. The reason they have remained this way is multifaceted, but it simplifies to a necessity. The slivers of Corona have always been oddly aggressive of the airspace, but with the most recent cataclysm having multiplied the numbers of Hive Bastions exponentially, they face a more existential threat.Unlike the other realms which have multiple hives that are in conflict with one another, the hives of Corona cooperate and act as a single unit. Many refuse to yield to the ancient enemy, while others say that they should cut their losses and live upon the chaotic world below.They frequently send mining expeditions to the central world, granting them an ample supply of mana crystals, making mana rifles more common a weapon than the lances.The Lightless serve as leaders of their society. The Perpetuals serve even in death, typically driven by a defiance against the Skep. The Sunderous typically serve as crew for the navy, a single navyman capable of filling dozens of roles at once. The Visionaries are trained to serve as advisors and strategists, accounting for the weftwinders spatial and temporal sunderings.The Werewolves, as in most realms, live on the world below, though they have concentrated in the equator, the poles, and beneath the surface. The surface outside of these areas is deemed too dangerous to properly colonize and settle upon. This makes Corona the only realm without a population of Werewolves who shift.The Hive Bastions of Corona do not conflict with one another, they instead cooperate. This is due to the Overlord's influence, subjugating the other bastions to it's will. This has let them form a unified whole that is infinitely more dangerous than their parts; the replication capacity of the queen, the self actualizing efficiency of the legion, the redundancies of the hivelord, the diversity of the first, the destruction of the monstrocity, the immortality of the gravemother, and the paradox of the weftwinder, all under the will of the overlord.They thrum spawned from the cores of hive bastions are initially free from the minds of the overlords, but they are soon eliminated. The smallest bastions, the ones without adaptations, are frequently raided as the bastions pass them by.The behaviors of the hives of Corona has always been characterized by an intelligence, and an aggressive commitment to aerial superiority. It has simply become more pronounced in recent histories. They have even threatened the dreadnaughts that are usually oh so insurmountable in the other realms.Terrain; Planet Padding (Nearby Planet) s+x The Core L^L Planar Nexus n Cascading Cataracts n Sunken Citadel s^2 Hall of Tagsin sn Crystal Quarry n^2 Unstable Frontier 4s Prismatic Vista 4n Mana Confluence s^3 Glimmervoid 6n Mana Confluence 6n Echoes of Eternity 6n (1+z+n) Skypost, Glimmerpost, Trenchpost L(1+z) (1+z+n) Sliver Hive (z+1) (1+z+n) Everywhere 2(z+1) Shimmerdrift Vale n^3 by s^2 Mirage Mesa s^2 by n^3 The Mycosynth Gardens (1+s) Capital City 2(1+s) Survivor's Encampment (2+s) Birthing Boughs (1+s) Valgavoth's Lair (2+s) Abundant Countryside 2(1+s) Hive; Thrumming Hivepool triangular(1+zx) Riptide Island sqrt(1+zx) Crawlspace 1 Hivestone 1 Animals; Germs Fungi Ants Gremlins
Eldrazi Sapients; Splinters Humans

item.title: Corona
War Machines
item.description:
Each of the realms have been in constant 'war' with the slivers of their homes, though, the exact nature varies. Some realms the war is active on both size, some realms they are both 'territorially defensive', and everything in-between. As such, the equipment the Humans were left with has seen good use, though limited reproductions, making every loss near permanent.
Heirlooms from before can include magnetic exoarmor, fueled by Moxite, granting them extremely enhanced physical abilities. Mechanically, it grants +4/+2, absorb 2, and ward {2} to the equipped creature.

Will-Forged Golem by Jason Felix
Some Heirlooms from before can include weapons made out of Etherium, mana guns and lightblades. The Lance, a handheld weapon, and the rifle, a two handed heaver variant.Lances condense the ambient mana into a bolt and acts as a sort of railgun for the bolt. It takes a number of seconds to charge a single shot equal to the number of the realm's colors plus one. Effectively, it deals 1 damage to target creature as a tap effect.

Kellan, the Kid by Magali Villeneuve
Rifles use mana crystals to overcharge the power into a potent spell, with a cooldown of triangular(X+1) * .5 seconds (.5 seconds for a crystal from Komas, 10.5 seconds for a crystal from Corona). Each mana crystal can sustain sixty five shots. X is equal to the number of colors of the mana crystal plus 1. Mechanically, equipped creature attacks to cast a copy of the spell with X as specified. Mana crystals are rare, being a resource each realm had to independently refine and manufacture, as such, the deployment of a Rifle is rare.

Taii Wakeen, Perfect Shot by David Auden Nash
Lightblades are hilts, polearms, or gauntlets that emit a constant blade of mana. Each color has their own 'motif' for the type of weapon their lightblades tend to manifest as. White manifests swords, blue manifests spears, black manifests curved blades such as scythes, sickles, and khopeshes, red manifests axes, and green manifests as claws or forearm mounted weaponry. Komas lacks lightblades, the ambient mana being too thin for form a blade in the first place. Mechanically, lightblades give +1/+0, enter attached to a creature, and gives that creature lifelink, first strike, deathtouch, trample, and/or haste until the end of the turn it entered. The keywords dependent on the colors of the lightblade.

Honored Knight-Captain by Forrest Imel

Treasure Hunt by Steve Prescott

Scourge for Eternity by Konstantin Porubov

Memorial Team Leader by Andrew Mar

Red Tiger Mechan by Simon Dominic
They have a tradition of using Cosmium to give themselves tattoos that glow an eldritch purplish white. One of the only implements of war that the knowledge to reproduce it has not been lost. They used to be more effective in the power they granted, but over the course of two specific Cataclysms, they have become but glimmers of their prior selves. The ratio to surface area and power gained is now, all things considered, abysmal.Below is what they were originally able to use, now, the area required for such spells is increased by two areas. Each tattoo correlates to a single cast of a single spell a day (Beginning of upkeep, creature becomes prepared). The color identity of the realm determines the color spells they can have access to. Even without spells, they grant 'substantial' benefits to the host.

Braided Net // Braided Quipu by Diego GisbertHumans have a number of relics from their past as military forces. These vehicles for their navy are heavily shielded and outfitted with powerful weaponry, however, they are also high maintenance, and many have fallen into disrepair. Effectively, the numbers in station, crew, and equip represents the number of personnel to appropriately operate that level of functionality. The number can be reduced by a factor of three if the entire crew is comprised of specially trained pilots.

Dawnsire, Sunstar Dreadnought by Jaime JonesEach realm has atleast a single dreadnought. These are certifiably superweapons, capable of obliterating the world they orbit...or atleast a portion of it. (12 mana Station 0+ Ward 6 "Protection from mana value 0 or less" Station 40+ Flying 3/3 Absorb 6 Ward 5 "Protection from mana value 1 or less" Station 80+ "Whenever this creature attacks, Dreadnought deals 8 damage to each creature opponents control." Absorb 5 Ward 4 "Protection from mana value 2 or less" 6/6 Station 160+ "Whenever this creature attacks, Dreadnought deals 1000 damage to up to one target creature or planeswalker." Absorb 4 Ward 3 "Protection from mana value 4 or less" 12/12 Station 320+ "Protection from mana value 5 or less" Absorb 3 Ward 2 24/24 Station 640+ "When this creature attacks, Dreadnought deals 2 damage to each opponent." Absorb 2 Ward 1 "Protection from mana value 6 or less" 48/48 Station 1280+ "When this creature attacks, Dreadnought deals 1 damage to each planeswalker opponents control. Each opponent that doesn't control a planeswalker loses 1 life instead." Absorb 1 "Protection from mana value 7 or less" 96/96)

Candela, Aegis of Adagia by Sergey GlushakovEach realm has atleast a handful of Cruisers. Smaller than the Dreadnoughts, less powerful as well, they are still extremely dangerous weapons nonetheless. (10 mana Station 0+ Ward 5 Station 20+ Flying 2.5/2.5 Absorb 5 Ward 4 "Protection from mana value 0 or less" Station 40+ "Whenever this creature attacks, Cruiser deals 4 damage to each creature opponents control." Absorb 4 Ward 3 "Protection from mana value 1 or less" 5/5 Station 80+ "Whenever this creature attacks, Cruiser deals 100 damage to up to one target creature or planeswalker." Absorb 3 Ward 2 "Protection from mana value 2 or less" 10/10 Station 160+ "Protection from mana value 3 or less" Absorb 2 Ward 1 20/20 Station 320+ "When this creature attacks, Cruiser deals 1 damage to each opponent." Absorb 1 "Protection from mana value 4 or less" 40/40)

Sky Skiff by Richard WrightEach realm has dozens of Destroyers. (8 mana Station 0+ Ward 4 Station 10+ Flying 2/2 Absorb 4 Ward 3 Station 20+ "Whenever this creature attacks, Destroyer deals 2 damage to each creature opponents control." Absorb 3 Ward 2 "Protection from mana value 0 or less" 4/4 Station 40+ "Whenever this creature attacks, Destroyer deals 10 damage to up to one target creature or planeswalker." Absorb 2 Ward 1 "Protection from mana value 1 or less" 8/8 Station 80+ "Protection from mana value 2 or less" Absorb 1 16/16)

Royal Talon Fighter Jet by Sean VoEach realm has a hundred or so Fighters, proportionally, they are small and cramped vehicles that can carry only a few people at a time and can be transported on Destroyers, Cruisers, and Dreadnaughts. (6 mana Station 0+ Ward 3 Station 5+ Flying 1.5/1.5 Absorb 3 Ward 2 Station 10+ "Whenever this creature attacks, Cruiser deals 1 damage to each creature opponents control." Absorb 2 Ward 1 3/3 Station 20+ "Whenever this creature attacks, Cruiser deals 1 damage to up to one target creature or planeswalker." Absorb 1 "Protection from mana value 0 or less" 6/6)

Thopter Squadron by Doug ChaffeeEach realm has a thousand or so Speeders, hover vehicles that rapidly travels great distances. Though they do have some degree of weaponry, they are better served as transport. (4 mana Ward 2 Crew 2+ Flying 1/1 Absorb 2 Ward 1 Crew 5+ "Whenever this creature attacks, Speeder deals .5 damage to each creature opponents control." Absorb 1 2/2)

Nautiloid Ship by Daniel Ljunggren
The vehicles of Splinters which they use to travel to and from their home bastion to the world below.

Hexgold Hoverwings by Kai CarpenterEach realm has a thousand or so Hoverwing Packs. These backpack sized armaments are attachable to even the exosuits and are used as personal maneuvering gear. They are not suited for long distance travel, but can be used for such a purpose if needed. It does have a maximum height it can lift off of the ground. The shields of the Packs offer some protection to their wearer. (2 mana Ward 1 Equip 1 : "Equipped creature has Flying, +.5/+.5, and Absorb 1")

Skitterbeam Battalion by Leon TukkerAutonomous Defense Weapon's Platforms are the main method which the Human bastion moons have protected themselves, despite being relatively non-maneuverable compared to vehicles, the Humans have had the most success in reproducing/manufacturing these vehicles. Effectively legs, a massive mana cannon, a chassis, and a sensor array. Compared to the complicated systems of flight in the other vehicles, the AI for the artillery is relatively simple. They even power themselves from the mana in the realms. (First Strike, Trample, Reach, "Ward - Pay life and mana equal to this creature’s toughness."; Prototype 4 1/1, Prototype 8 2/2, Prototype 12 4/4, 16 8/8; "When this creature enters, if you cast it, create two tokens that are copies of it.")

Hermitic Nautilus by Logan FelicianoA living vehicle which Splinters use to explore beneath and upon the surface of the central worlds.

item.title: Pasted image 20260627121725
Pasted image 20260627015518
Splinter
item.description:
Splinters are odd humanoids, highly social, yet oddly solitary. They are descendants of Slivers that 'evolved individualism' as best they can figure, around the time the Fomori disappeared, in an alternate reality where Humans were never left behind. Overtime, they have lost a majority of their 'sliveriness', they barely have a hivemind, they are barely shapeshifters, they are humanoid in form, and some Humans argue that their limited emotional ranges barely qualifies them for sapience. The Splinters often regard the sliver hives in a similar manner to a human and a chimpanzee, not the same and not the same level of sentience/sapience generally, yet something that can definitely be dangerous if provoked.
On the hivemind point, they have a thought-weft. They are able to telepathically communicate with one another and share emotions, but not share their thoughts, memories, or experiences. It also lends them a general sense of the direction and distance of the nearest sliver, which being no longer in any of the hives, they avoid. As well as a far more accurate sense of the direction and distance of other splinters.On the humanoid point, beyond that, they look off. Their bodies are covered in chitinous plates (grey with marbling of the realm's colors), their eyes are more akin to gemstones that are the same color of the realm's colors mixed together, they have "hair" that is more like the squirming tentacles of a jellyfish or polyp than true hair, and they have cloaks of leathery flesh that is oddly similar to tanned human skin when at rest. They do not have ears or mouths that are visible, rather they seem to have plates around such structures, either protecting those structures or giving the illusion that such structures exist, it is not clear which is true. The cloak of flesh and their mouth/ear guards mimic clothes ay a first glance. Compared to normal slivers, these adaptations were made over the course of years rather than moments.On the shapeshifter point, what little they can shapeshift depends drastically upon the realm they inhabit, though one trait they all share is the ability to change the texture and coloration of the outside of their fleshy cloaks much like an octopus can change their skin's texture and color.Splinters are relegated to an aberrant hive bastion generally, atleast new ones are. This bastion moon is much like the normal hive bastions, but it has several notable differences. The first of which is the liquid metal sphere that acts as the core to their bastion is now a structure that is more tree like and fleshy, pockets and sacks of gestating Splinters pockmarking the surface of the trunk.Unlike the Humans, they will periodically have numbers of their populations leave their bastion moon for the world they orbit. They have begun to settle the world, progressing more and faster than the Humans have over their history on the realms.Also unlike Humans, who's emotions are relatively unaffected by the color identity of the realm they are on, Splinter's emotions are highly influenced by the identity of the realm they are on. These emotions are an artifact of their 'sliverness', having traded physical adaptations for purely emotional ones.

item.title: War Web
item.description:
During the Fomori-Eldrazi War, the Fomori of the Multiverse travelled across the chaos wall frequently. To facilitate the supply lines for this war, they built staging grounds into the chaos wall, spaces between the finite infinity between the multiverse and the edge.
These pockets stabilized the chaos, concentrated it into mana, and then concentrated it into matter. A self perpetuating Engine of expansion that served as staging grounds, relay stations, and recharging stations. Each one aligned with different colors of mana to serve as perfect 'batteries' for different machines and purposes.
The Fomori had many war machines, but with the war against the Eldrazi and Ixalan both going simultaneously, they implemented an unusual force to safeguard these locations from the near constant invasions of the Eldrazi. They used a peculiar organism they found within both the edge and the multiverse, Slivers.
Making artifacts to control their hiveminds, they were able to have the Slivers reliably safeguard the planar portals and eternity columns of the artificial worlds from their bastion moons, allowing themselves, their ships, and their war machines to pass through uncontested, while aggressively fighting and adapting to the Eldrazi menace. A strategy not unlike the methods employed by Volrath to guard the Legacy, but on a more grand scale.The Fomori did not come into these pockets entirely devoid of other forms of life. Bringing in the slivers and coming in themselves, they had various hitchhikers, ancestors of the flora and fauna that can be found in the pockets today. Though, as they were not intentionally seeded with life nor designed to host life other than Slivers, so major forms of life are few and not particularly diverse.
Since the Fomori disappeared, the pockets expanded, life continued to adapt, and the concentrations of mana warped the landscape. As they expanded, they weakened the wall in specific nebulous points, which has facilitated the crossing between the two occasionally. Such as Tezzeret entering into the Edge.
Various unintended side effects of these pockets have become prominent over the millennia, leylines from the Edge slowly grows Moxite along them, while leylines from the Multiverse slowly grows Etherium along them. They act as a sort of 'self-repairing' 'natural' wire that conducts the mana into the central 'engine'. The Eastern Pole is predominantly where the multiversal leylines come from, while the Western Pole is predominantly where the Exoversal leylines come from. Overtime, the denizens of the realms have mined and harvested the materials, which has resulted in a higher concentration in the center of the realms, where the leylines converge and feed into the 'engine'.
The souls of the Slivers, along with the various modifications they made to their biology, were designed to use the leylines to feed into the 'engine'. The dead have their souls shredded, mere remnants at best remain, while the rest travels along the leylines. The edges, emotions, memories, non-sliver form/identity, all snag along the leylines, forming and growing cosmium deposits along them. Unlike the veins/wires of metal that grow from the outside inwards, these veins grow from the inside outwards as a sort of patina on the wires.Due to the constant invasions of Eldrazi and the hyper territorial Slivers, major settlements have never appeared on any of the realms outside of their home bastion moon. There are small pockets of society on many of the realm's main worlds, but that is just about all there is in nations.
These realms were staging grounds for military forces, those forces were Human auxilaries, foreign legions, and/or penal legions. Outfitted with exosuits fueled by moxite, mana guns and light blades made of etherium, and various modifications from cosmium infusions. They were left here when the Fomori disappeared, and it wasn't long before the slivers began to view them as threats and began to attack them. Those that remain have maintained only a shadow of their past glories, with Cataclysms, major catastrophes from the multiverse and exoverse, having modified random fractions of their populations, creating further distinct sub populations of Human.
The most recent cataclysm has resulted in the arrival of Splinters, beings who had evolved independence in a version of the realms where humans were never left behind.Oddly, Eldrazi incursions have become less frequent in the past decade or so.(For the purposes of anyone who looks into patterns, each integer is multiplied by 10 to make a number of kilometers.)
(s is the number of legendary slivers in WUBRG there are, n is the number of unique arts for the legendary slivers in WUBRG (minus 1 for legion (crayon) and plus 1 for queen (duelmasters)), L is the number of locus there are, z is the number of legendary slivers in the color identity, x is the number of sliver cards in the color identity)The central planet of each realm rotates. A full rotation lasts twenty five hours exactly, regardless of the size of the world, each day and night lasting six hours and fifteen minutes exactly, having a total of two of both per rotation. When the worlds face the multiverse or exoverse, it is day, while they face the chaos wall, it is night. The planes and star systems of the multiverse and exoverse respectively form stars that get filtered through the exosphere of the realm into sunlight.Due to this rotation, they have a warmer equator and colder poles, their poles are trapped in perpetual night while the equators are trapped in near perpetual daylight.
On the horizon, beyond the skies, through the first two layers of the Exospheres lies a basic translation of the blind eternities into something real and definable. Shadows dance across the skies through this translation, some quick, others languid, but all are the forms of Eldrazi within the Chaos Wall. These shadows are best analogous in appearance to dark clouds, higher than clouds should be, capable of moving behind the stars.Often, these shadows will move into the exosphere and result in an eldrazi drone or two. This occurs frequently enough that the realmic denizens are unaware of the fact that Eldrazi are not normal forms of fauna.The rest of the life on the realms are evolutions of ants, microbial life, and fungal growths.Orbiting each world, within their atmospheres, are small moons. Some have many, some have few. Generated and expanded by the engines when particular evolutionary triggers are met, resulting in a great variance between the number of bastion moons around any given realm, even amongst the same strata.Within the center of each bastion is a liquid metal sphere, a structure made to sustain that particular hive's population, spawning a continual stream of thrum, spawning quicker the larger these cores are.Slowly expanded, one of the bastion moons was designed to house a human garrison, in the event that the realms were invaded and the slivers fell. Recently, they have been manually expanded to house those who are displaced by Sliver aggression.The Exosphere is composed of three distinct spheres.The first sphere, the Glimmervoid, like a fog of silvery dust, translates mana into matter and accumulates it into the realm. It also serves to recycle matter that enters into it as material deposited into the realms.The second sphere, the Mana Confluence, is nothing but light, heat, mana, and radiation. This sphere translates the aether from the blind eternities into mana and energy.The third and final sphere, the Echoes of Eternity, a shadowy scape that can drive those who look upon it mad. Shadows of the true forms of eldrazi move within, glimpses of the exoverse and multiverse can be seen through it, and there is destructive aether that annihilates matter infusing it all. This sphere somehow translating the blind eternities of the chaos wall into a definable tangible reality, albeit, not a hospitable one.The more colors of mana that needs to be processed or manufactured, the less stable the process is and the larger the exosphere needs to be to facilitate proper expansion.Using the following equations, you can determine the size of any given realm, the result is in kilometers and includes the exosphere.n = 18 → 18s = 8 → 8L = 3 → 3 Ex(y) = (y + 1) * 3 * n → Ex(y)Co(x) = s + x + L^L + n + s^2 + s*n → Co(x) Zero(x) = (Co(x) + n^2 + 4*s + Ex(0))*10 → Zero(x)One(x) = (Co(x) + n^2 + 4*s + 4*n + s^3 + Ex(1))*10 → One(x)Two(x) = (Co(x) + n^2 + Ex(2))*10 → Two(x)Three(x) = (Co(x) + Ex(3))*10 → Three(x)Four(x) = (Co(x) + Ex(4))*10 → Four(x)Five(x) = (Co(x) + n^2 + 4*s + 4*n + s^3 + Ex(5))*10 → Five(x)The bottom of the multiverse lies a single realm, that of no color.
Komas - A realm devoid of colors of mana, unique for its mechanical Slivers. The first strata of the chaos wall's holes are the realms who's identities possess only a single color of mana.
Lumos - A realm of white mana
Souse - A realm of blue mana
Nocturne - A realm of black mana
Ignis - A realm of red mana
Sett - A realm of green mana The second strata of the chaos wall's holes are the realms who's identities possess a pair of colors of mana.
Unnamed 1 - A realm of wu mana
Ioxis - A realm of wb mana
Unnamed 2 - A realm of wr mana
Iethol - A realm of wg mana
Noxou - A realm of ub mana
Zyxkal - A realm of ur mana
Unnamed 3 - A realm of ug mana
Montresor - A realm of br mana
Unnamed 4 - A realm of bg mana
Unnamed 5 - A realm of rg mana The third strata of the chaos wall's holes are the realms who's identities possess three colors of mana.
Hytropo - A realm of wub
Unnamed 6 - A realm of wur
Unnamed 7 - A realm of wug
Unnamed 8 - A realm of wbr
Unnamed 9 - A realm of wbg
Unnamed 10 - A realm of wrg
Unnamed 11 - A realm of ubr
Unnamed 12 - A realm of ubg
Unnamed 13 - A realm of urg
Kithestra - A realm of brg The fourth strata of the chaos wall's holes are the realms who's identities lack a single color of mana.
Unnamed 14 - A realm of wubr
Unnamed 15 - A realm of wubg
Unnamed 16 - A realm of wurg
Unnamed 17 - A realm of wbrg
Unnamed 18 - A realm of ubrg The top of the multiverse lies a singular realm who's identity is all encompassing.
Corona - A realm of wubrg

item.title: Human
item.description:
"The old stories claim that our ancestors conquered worlds, they bled a star to vanquish the dark, they fought that which extinguished the light. We served gods who some believe abandoned us...what is it that you believe?" - UnknownHumans were once Auxiliary forces for the Fomori in their multiple wars. Notably serving as Foreign Legions against the Eldrazi of the Exoverse, and as Penal Legions against the Vampires of Ixalan. Though, due to time and the Cataclysms, their once rigid militarism has given way to multiple different cultures, shaped by the mana of their home. They have, generally, retained working amounts of Exosuits, Managuns, Lightblades, knowledge of Cosmium infusions onto their own persons, as well as War Machines and vehicles of old. For some reason, those affected by the Cataclyms do not usually seek to use, maintain, or make these artifacts for themselves.
Generally speaking, Humans are equivalent to an unadapted Slivers in strength, durability, and speed. (1/1s)Due to the Cataclysms, there are several 'aberrant' populations that seem to rely on their altered capacities rather than the ones they were left by the Fomori. Not that they don't acknowledge them as useful, but that they seem to have a strange aversion to the technologies. Not to say that they won't use them, but more that they have a preference to simply not.Beings born from the first cataclysm and improved by the second, they are near humans, their forms monochrome, their blood gone, their bodies cold, their hearts replaced with magical singularities (the photon collars the same colors of their realm), and they became biologically immortal. They can see the vitality of others as something they describe to be a 'spark of light', which they can not see in one another, hence they have given themselves the moniker of 'lightless'. An odd fact or feature of these beings is that their fertility rates are truly abysmal. When they first came into being, they had a fertility rate of ~.815 children per female (within a period of a normal Human lifespan and a normal fertility rate of 1.63), however, the second cataclysm worsened the issue, reducing the rate to ~.271. Due to their unageing nature, and low birth rates, a majority of the Lightless who exist today existed during the time of the first cataclysm.They have the ability to teleport around their homerealm, sort of consuming themselves within their own singularity, until the singularity vanishes, reappearing somewhere else with a small burst of wind and a pop. (At the beginning of your upkeep, you may exile this creature, if you do, return it to the battlefield under it's owner's control.)The souls of those who die near them (within a handful of meters) are inexorably consumed and shredded by the singularity within their chests. A portion of the vitality given to the Lightless, while a portion of it is expelled as minorly harmful levels of radiation via their photon collars. (Whenever this creature or another creature or artifact you control dies, target opponent gains 1 rad counter and you gain 1 life.)Those who are willing can consciously choose to feed themselves to the singularity of a Lightless, their matter enhancing and reinforcing the matter of the Lightless. This process has granted a number of lightless strength and durability beyond the imaginations of us mere mortals. However, this strengthening has an odd quirk, it disappears when they teleport, which has made them view the convenient transportation as a last resort, something to save their lives rather than as a preferred travel method. (Whenever you sacrifice another creature or artifact, put a +1/+1 counter on this creature.)

Lightless Evangel by Viko MenezesBeings born from the third cataclysm, their hair became a dust-like material akin to a nebulae and their irises became starfields.These people are the only ones who survive death upon the realms. When they die, their souls shed their mortal flesh and becomes something immaterial, yet solidly real. Ghosts and Echoes of what they once were. (When this creature dies, exile it and create a token copy of it, except the token is a spirit instead of a human, it has a base power and toughness of 1/1, and it gains absorb 1.) They are stripped of most emotions and drives, leaving only the last or strongest emotion or drive they had in life. Able to pursue their newfound purpose eternally, as they lose the need to eat, drink, and sleep. Over time, some can overcome the scars of death, regranting them a mind similar to what they possessed in life.Dissipating one of these echoes is a task few try to undertake, not only because it is difficult, but because they retain their knowledge and memories of life, making them windows to ages past, some of which may have little to no written records.

Flitterstep Eidolon by Chase StoneBeings born from the fourth cataclysm, they have strange shadows, both shadows that go away from and towards sources of light.They can have these shadows animate into physical bodies that look and act just as substantial as the Sundrous who spawned them, looking near identical to the individual, changing appearance based on actions the Sundrous may have taken earlier in the day, that would still lead to this moment. They, and the Sundrous, each lack all shadows. The Sundrous and their 'others' each take actions the Sundrous was likely to take simultaneously, before disappearing shortly afterwards. (Myriad) The others are autonomous, reacting to stimulus and situations like the Sundrous would. A small fraction of the memories going to the Sundrous, about ten minutes worth, divided amongst every Other.

Legion Loyalty by Michal IvanBeings born from the fifth cataclysm, their eyes glow and reflect light, objects, and scenes not present.They possess the ability to see into the future as shadows, the distance and accuracy of these visions can be increased with effort and training, though there is no insignificant amount of skill involved in the process. ( one to five {X}: Scry X or Fateseal X.)Those who do not train can be plagued with improbable visions of the world seconds in advance from it not happening, which can cause what is perceived as schizophrenic symptoms. Such individuals are best served by being forced to train their control over their ability.The most common refinement of this ability is narrowing down the shadows they see to those of likely futures or hours ahead, refining further to most likely futures or weeks ahead, and finally refining with the most skilled Visionaries to seeing the future that will occur rather than what could occur or seeing years ahead. There is typically a trade off between accuracy (likelihood) and depth (distance from the present) that these shadows display as.

Memory Drain by PINDURSKIBeings born from the seventh cataclysm, they grew horns and antlers, eyes like a cat's, their hair color changed to a color or colors related to their home realm's color identity, slightly pointed ears, and they grew tusks not unlike an elephant's in form.While in daylight, be it direct or soft, they appear more or less human, with the above changes. Though they are more innately magical and physically inclined. (2/2s. Annihilator 1, Absorb 1, Phased 2 (During your combat, the battlefield is divided into two sectors. If a creature isn’t assigned to a sector, its controller assigns it to one. Opponents assign first. Creatures have protection from every creature not in their sector until the end of combat. If multiple creatures have the Phased keyword, divide the battlefield into a number of sectors equal to the highest number of Phased.), Flanking, Enrage - Deal 2 damage to any target, and +1/+1. Human.) Realms with equitorial bands of desert have populations of perpetual dayformed Werewolves.
While in night light, the subtle shimmer of the exosphere, they become wolf-like monstrosities. They become larger, their horns and tusks grow out, they become covered in fur, they grow claws, fangs, and the like. In short, they become werewolves with certain eccentricities. Their personalities shift to becoming far more violent, aggressive, and hungry. They socialize with one another, but not with any other variety of Human, Splinter, or other similar being. Their memories, cognition, and personalities are entirely divorced from their daylight and eclipsed forms. (8/8s. Annihilator 4, Absorb 4, Phased 8, 'Flanking, Flanking, Flanking, Flanking', Enrage - Deal 8 damage to any target, and +4/+4. Human creature type replaced by Werewolf.) Realms with arctic poles have populations of perpetual nightformed Werewolves.While deprived of or in the presence of both daylight and nightlight, they become pitiful creatures. They look emaciated, their horns shrink and grow becoming asymmetric and gangly, their tusks shrink to little more than slightly longer canines, their upper canines grow out to match the length of their lower canines, their eyes become a normal Human's, their hair loses their luster, and they lose their innate arcane abilities. Psychologically, they become erratic, isolationist, and generally paranoid, specifically that everyone is trying to kill them and that they will die in the sunlight and nightlight. Unlike the dayform and nightform, they lack consistent contextual memories, as this form usually does not occur for long if at all in the Werewolf's life, at best appearing briefly during dawn and dusk, only to 'cease' as the day rises or the night falls. They grow claws and their ears change to something wolf-like. (1/2 / 1/2s. Human Werewolves.) In the realms of the 0th, 1st, and 5th strata, there are populations of perpetual eclipseformed werewolves who live under the surface of the central world.

Lunar Frenzy by Alix Branwyn

item.title: Cataclysms
item.description:
The realms lying between the Multiverse and the Exoverse simultaneously, major changes and events between the two ripples the wall, causing changes within a seemingly random subset of the population.These events, as far as the denizens of the realms are concerned, are random. And they are catastrophic. The entire realm shakes as if suffering from an ever increasingly cataclysmic earthquake, winds tear through the landscapes via horrific tornados, the central hive gains a blind bloodlust trying to slaughter every living thing each individual encounters, including themselves, and geomagnetic storms, infused with mana, rock what little technology the denizens utilize. This is combined with a number of rad counters onto each individual equal to the result (as radiation rockets through the leylines as a sort of feedback) and there is an R value for radio blackout equal to the Kp result as well.C is equal to the number cataclysms the realms have experienced, counting the one they are currently experiencing.Earthquakes last TimeFactor milliseconds, Winds last TimeFactor seconds, Geomagnetic storms last TimeFactor minutes, and Slivers remain with mindless bloodlust for TimeFactor hours.These events have become more violent with each series of ripples, enough so that the sliver hives are beginning to take close to a year to recover from the damages to the environments and their internal hive structures.
Creation of Point Prime
When the first Supervoid was born in the exoverse, it rippled across the realms. A portion of the population became monochrome, their hearts became replaced with magical singularities, they became biologically immortal, and they gained the power to teleport around their home realms. They consume a fraction of the 'light' the souls, of those who dies near them, becoming rejuvenated while emitting a minor amount of radiation. These beings seem to have difficulties increasing their numbers compared to normal Humans. Sotheran Collapse
When the thousandth Supervoid was born in the exoverse, it rippled across the realms. Those who were previously changed changed further, gaining the ability to reinforce their own matter with the matter of another who is willing, becoming stronger and tougher beyond what many could even imagine. Time Spiral
When the past of Dominaria crashed into it's present simultaneously, it rippled across the realms. A portion of the humans had their hair change into a nebulae-esque material, while their irises became starfields. When these individuals die, they remained, their spirit becoming an entity as real and tangible as they were when alive, but altered. No longer needing to eat, drink, breath, or sleep, born with an overriding emotion, and becoming much much harder to kill. Planar Chaos
When the adjacent presents of Dominaria coexisted together, it rippled across the realms. A portion of the humans began to cast multiple shadows in the presence of light. They gained the ability to temporarily split themselves into multiple variations of themselves, each real and physical, taking actions that the individual considered to take simultaneously. Then, all but one present collapses back into one. Future Sight
When the possible futures of Dominaria washed over the present, it rippled across the realms. A portion of the humans had their eyes glow with a strange source less glimmer. They were each able to see potential futures as shadowy echoes of what could be. With some minor focus, they can see only the most likely futures. With extreme skill and training, they have the ability to see what will happen rather than what may happen. The Great Mending
When planeswalkers were reduced from gods to merely powerful wizards, it rippled across the realms. The efficacy of cosmium infusions degraded significantly. Increasing the area required for the simplest of spells by a category. Eventide, Morningtide, Eclipsed
When the balance of the Great Aurora was returned, it rippled across the realms. A portion of the humans had their hair change color to that of their home realm. These humans gained minor arcane powers and slightly enhanced physicality during the day, at night, their magic and physicality would become elevated by magnitudes as they became werewolves, while away from the day or night skies, they became a weaker version of a typical human. Fraught with conflicting psychologies, weak constitutions, and generally erratic behavior. These 'eclipsed' versions grow fangs, claws, and wolf-like ears. The Eventide, Morningtide, and Eclipse are each individual cataclysms. The Great Pruning
When planeswalkers were deprived of their sparks reducing many of them to normal wizards, it rippled across the realms. The efficacy of cosmium infusions was further reduced. Increasing the area required for the simplest of spells by a category.
Creation of the Echoverse
When Jace created an alternate multiverse, a portion of the humans became replaced. They were fractured into Splinters, aware of an alternate history and reality than what they are currently experiencing. Similar in disposition to the human they replaced, even similar names, but undoubtedly a different individual altogether. This cataclysm also significantly altered the slivers and their hive bastions, creating near exponentially more of them than were previously, making their adaptations more extreme, and making them separate entities.

Slivers
item.description:
Slivers have four methods of reproduction, one from the multiverse, one from the exoverse, one unique to these [pocket realms](War Web), one shared between the multiverse and exoverse, and one introduced with the cataclysm of the Echoverse.The first, unique to these worlds, is autogenesis. The liquid metal pool in the heart of every hive bastion, periodically spawns thrum. The number spawned can increase over the course of centuries and millennia, initially having been one at what we would call 'midnight', the end of a realm's rotation.

Thrumming Hivepool by Rob ReyThe second, the multiversal method, is transfiguration. Non-shapeshifters who find themselves within the Hive Bastions will find themselves slowly transformed into Slivers themselves. This process is extremely long, but takes exponentially less time the closer one is to the core of the bastion. Going from effectively centuries to mere minutes. Proximity to the core is the only factor that changes the rate of transformations, but they can cause physiological and psychological issues in the victim of the process. Issues which can result in death, disabilities, and/or insanity. The quicker the process is, the more painful it is to endure. It is this factor that has prevented successful excursions into the hive bastions to shut them down, take control of, or otherwise modify the sliver populations of the realms.

Metamorphose by Ron SpencerThe third, the exoversal method, is oviparity. Specifically, utilizing externally fertilized parasitic ovipositors. Basically, xenomorphing people. Stabbing them with a specific tendrils that can extend from their beak-like 'faces' and implanting an embryo. It uses the genetics of the host to determine the color alignment and future adaptation (if any), technically the external fertilization is this process of utilizing the host's dna. If you are, at the time of being implanted, classified as a blue black being, then the sliver who emerges would either be colorless or blue black. The host will be unconscious for a day, due to adjacently injected toxins that quells the immune system and causes the host to believe they simply fell asleep rather than having been injected with an embryo, then it will develop over the course of ten days in the host, then spend five days after violently bursting from the host's torso to mature fully into a Thrum or fully adapted Sliver.

Syphon Sliver by Johann Bodin
The fourth, the shared method, is parthenogenesis. Slivers are capable of laying eggs via asexual reproduction. This can only be done while the individual's cortisol levels are low to non-existent, otherwise known as the individual feeling safe and secure. There are, of course, exceptions or modifications to this if the hiveform is specialized for reproduction in some capacity. Three notable examples being Sliver Queen, The First Sliver, and Hatchery Sliver. The queen can simply do this regardless of state and hatchery overdrives this process.

Hatchery Sliver by Svetlin VelinovThe fifth, the method gained from the creation of the Echoverse, is a form of spontaneous generation. Bastions have appeared with populations composed of exclusively a single adaptation, when bastions without a commanding ego creates a new adaptation, the adaptation is purged and a new bastion is generated filled with that adaptation. When a bastion with a commanding ego creates a new adaptation, they do not purge the adaptation but a new bastion is created still. The creation of a bastion this way makes the air around it within x kilometers sounds as if the sky itself is being torn asunder and shattered. This 'Echoing Genesis' results in some highly specialized hives, and has since caused the hives to no longer be united amongst themselves, an introduction of division in return for increased populations.

Mirage Mirror by Craig J Spearing

Engine
item.description:
The Planar Portal that enables travel to the multiverse and [other realms](War Web).
The Eternity Column that enables travel to the exoverse and the other realms (if you know the location of the others).
It coordinates the leylines from both realities towards the center, funnels souls into itself, it facilitates the stabilization of the chaos wall into something substantially real, the expansion of the realms, and the drive of the sliver hives to protect it.
The engine itself is underground, a miniature sun within a spherical cave, branches, roots, and veins of moxite, cosmium, and etherium reaching out from the mechanical structures that surrounds it to grasp, reinforce, and cage the nascent star. These tendrils shift and sway, as if the metals were living and moving about.In most realms, the cave the star is in gives it ample space, radiation, mana, and molten dust torrentially shifts and flows around the space between the core and the rest of the world. The violence of the energies within the stellar core is so violent that, merely the light itself, is enough to melt and annihilate most it comes into contact with.In many of the realms, machinery still holds firm as a lining around the plasmic core. In some of those, the lining transitions to tunnels of stone, which travels through a vast quantity of rock underneath the surface of these worlds.
